mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, compressing valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.05) relative to baseline during rate cuts, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations, triggering negative equity returns within 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 55% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to predict direction with accuracy above 52% over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized domestic demand growth, which drives corporate revenue expectations higher and produces positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to generate positive 60-day returns in > 60% of cases, or interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Contraction in the index of industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects supply-side bottlenecks and reduced manufacturing output, causing immediate negative market repricing over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in > 58% of instances when INDPRO declines during tightening periods, or the mechanism shows zero predictive power in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPIAUCSL reading exceeding 3.0% triggers expectations of restrictive monetary policy, which increases discount rates and forces negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of triggered months, or the signal directional accuracy drops below 50% in a walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic capacity and rising wage income, which supports corporate earnings growth and drives positive quarterly equity market performance.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show no positive correlation with payroll growth, or Rank IC remains negative over consecutive 2-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between declining real GDP growth and rising real personal consumption expenditures indicates unsustainable consumer borrowing, which triggers a negative market correction over the next month as debt servicing costs rise.
failure_condition: The interaction term between GDPC1 and RSAFS fails to predict negative returns, or positive 20-day returns occur in > 55% of divergence episodes.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the industrial production index during a post-recession recovery phase signals rapid capacity utilization and strong business investment, generating immediate positive equity returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in > 60% of instances when INDPRO rises during recovery regimes, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample tests.
